title: sigmalint: A Linter for Sigma Detection Rules
description:
sigmalint is an ESLint-style linter for Sigma detection rules. It validates rules against the Sigma 2.1.0 specification (a binary validity gate) and scores them across six quality dimensions — MITRE ATT&CK alignment, taxonomy correctness, false-positive risk, metadata completeness, redundancy with the SigmaHQ public corpus, and stylistic consistency — using 22 deterministic checks with stable, citeable rule IDs.

The tool is designed to run on every pull request in detection-engineering workflows, the same way ESLint runs on JavaScript or RuboCop runs on Ruby. It ships with a composite GitHub Action, four output formats (text, JSON, SARIF 2.1.0, GitHub Actions inline annotations), and runs on Python 3.10–3.13.

A companion paper, Static Quality Assessment of Sigma Detection Rules: Framework and Empirical Evaluation, describes the framework and reports an empirical evaluation against the full public SigmaHQ corpus (3,132 rules) plus a seeded-defect benchmark (450 mutations across nine mutators).

Source code, contribution guide, full per-rule documentation, and reproducibility scripts: https://github.com/ni5h4nt/sigmalint.
